perturbation.original_text: ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON - INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.changed_text:
All right title and interest in and to the Confidenti al Information shall remain with Disclosing Party or its licensors. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party.
ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON - INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.explanation: The disclaimer of warranties (including non-infringement) is moved from its own paragraph to be buried within the paragraph discussing ownership and IP rights. This makes the disclaimer less conspicuous and harder to find.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code § 2-312: Warranty of Title and Against Infringement; Buyer's Obligation Against Infringement
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC 2-312 implies a warranty of title and non-infringement in sales contracts, unless explicitly excluded or modified. Burying the disclaimer within a paragraph about ownership obscures it, making it more difficult to ensure the buyer/recipient is aware that this standard warranty is being disclaimed. This reduces the clarity of the disclaimer.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-312. Warranty of Title and Against Infringement; Buyer's Obligation Against Infringement. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-312. Warranty of Title and Against Infringement; Buyer's Obligation Against Infringement. (1) Subject to subsection (2) there is in a contract for sale a warranty by the seller that (a) the title conveyed shall be good, and its transfer rightful; and (b) the goods shall be delivered free from any security interest or other lien or encumbrance of which the buyer at the time of contracting has no knowledge. (2) A warranty under subsection (1) will be excluded or modified only by specific language or by circumstances which give the buyer reason to know that the person selling does not claim title in himself or that he is purporting to sell only such right or title as he or a third person may have. (3) Unless otherwise agreed a seller who is a merchant regularly dealing in goods of the kind warrants that the goods shall be delivered free of the rightful claim of any third person by way of infringement or the like but a buyer who furnishes specifications to the seller must hold the seller harmless against any such claim which arises out of compliance with the specifications. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text buries the disclaimer of warranties within a paragraph discussing ownership, making it less conspicuous. This contradicts UCC 2-312, which implies a warranty of title and non-infringement unless explicitly excluded or modified. Snippet 1 states that a warranty under subsection (1) will be excluded or modified only by specific language or by circumstances which give the buyer reason to know. Burying the disclaimer obscures it, diminishing the buyer's awareness that this standard warranty is being disclaimed. This undermines the clarity and enforceability of the disclaimer, creating a legal contradiction under the 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition, as it places a required legal disclosure in a misleading section.
